meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve overwhelmed by AI tools (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome overwhelmed by AI tools. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: overwhelmed by AI tools

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Master Your First Real-World SaaS Project
content.seo_description: Stop feeling overwhelmed by AI tools. Learn to debug like a senior engineer using Antigravity and Claude Code in this intermediate-level student tutorial.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing every new AI tool. Instead, build a systematic debugging workflow using Claude Code for terminal-based reasoning and Antigravity for architectural visualization. This combination bridges the gap between raw code errors and system-level logic, allowing you to resolve complex bugs while building a robust, production-ready portfolio project.
content.intro:
The modern developer landscape feels like a digital minefield. You open your IDE, and suddenly you are bombarded by a dozen AI assistants, each promising to solve your problems, yet leaving you paralyzed by choice and fragmented context. The cognitive load is immense, and for a student, this 'AI fatigue' is the single greatest barrier to shipping your first production-ready application.

But here is the secret: senior engineers don't use more tools; they use better workflows. By mastering the synergy between Claude Code’s intent-driven execution and Antigravity’s structural visualization, you stop fighting the compiler and start orchestrating your code. It is time to move beyond simple prompts and start building with the precision of a professional.

steps.title: Establish the Architectural Baseline
steps.description: Before debugging, visualize your current codebase structure to identify where the logic breaks. Use Antigravity to map your dependency graph.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project directory and generate a dependency graph. Highlight potential circular dependencies or bloated modules that could be causing runtime instability.

steps.title: Contextual Error Isolation
steps.description: Don't feed the whole codebase to an AI. Use Claude Code to isolate the specific stack trace and relevant files to prevent hallucination.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the relationship between the current failing service and the database schema defined in the schema.prisma file.

steps.title: Iterative Logic Correction
steps.description: Apply a 'Think-Then-Execute' pattern. Ask the AI to propose a fix based on your project's existing design patterns before applying changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the architectural map, suggest a refactor that resolves the current null pointer exception while maintaining consistency with our existing error handling patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask "Propose a patch for the identified bug that adheres to our project's existing functional programming style."

steps.title: Automated Regression Testing
steps.description: Ensure your fix doesn't break other parts of the app by running a targeted verification suite.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of integration tests that specifically target the module we just patched to ensure no regressions were introduced.

steps.title: Documentation and Knowledge Capture
steps.description: Document the bug fix in your project's ADR (Architecture Decision Record) to build your professional portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the debugging process we just completed into a concise ADR entry, focusing on the root cause and the architectural reasoning behind the fix.

pro_tips: Always force the AI to explain its reasoning BEFORE it suggests code changes to avoid 'lazy coding' habits.
Use Claude Code to generate your README.md based on the actual code state to ensure your portfolio documentation is never outdated.
Treat your AI session like a code review; if the AI suggests a solution you don't understand, ask it to explain the 'why' using analogies.
Keep your context window clean by periodically clearing the chat history once a major logical milestone is reached.
Use Antigravity to visualize your progress; seeing a cleaner dependency graph is a major motivator for finishing your project.
content.next_steps: After completing this tutorial, deploy your project to Vercel or Railway and share the link on your LinkedIn to demonstrate your ability to handle complex debugging workflows.
content.conversion_block: Stop feeling like a spectator in your own development process. Master these tools, build your confidence, and ship the project that gets you hired. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
